key_insight: The study uses thermo-mechanical simulation and comparative screening to identify conditions that promote flash formation during inertia friction welding of FGH98 powder superalloy.
engineering_relevance: Its findings can help optimize weld quality and process stability for high-performance superalloy joints by reducing defect-prone parameter choices.
method: Built a coupled thermo-mechanical simulation of inertia friction welding.
Compared candidate conditions to screen for flash formation behavior.
Focused on FGH98 powder superalloy as the target material system.
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a journal article in Welding in the World centered on simulation-based comparative screening rather than reporting experimental metrics in the provided text.
watch_item: Results may be process- and material-specific, so direct transfer to other superalloys or welding setups should be validated experimentally.
paper_title: Thermo-mechanical simulation and comparative screening of flash formation in inertia friction welding of FGH98 powder superalloy
